originalTitle: 'Peace is a gradual thing': How land, cattle and identity fuel a deadly Nigerian conflict
title: Making Friends and Sharing Land in Plateau State, Nigeria!
content:
Hello from Sunny Africa!

Hello, amazing friends! It's your happy African news friend, Aisha, bringing you wonderful stories from our incredible continent! Today, we're flying over to a big and beautiful country called Nigeria. Nigeria is in West Africa, full of exciting places and many, many kind people. It's a place where children laugh and play, just like you! Sometimes, even in the most wonderful places, grown-ups can have big disagreements. We're going to talk about a special part of Nigeria called Plateau State. In Plateau State, people are learning how important it is to share and be kind to each other, so everyone can live happily and safely.

What is the Big Problem?

Imagine you and your friends want to play with the same toy. Sometimes, it's hard to decide who gets to play first, right? Well, in Plateau State, grown-ups sometimes have disagreements that are like big, serious arguments. These arguments can make people sad and sometimes even scared. It's not about toys, though; it's about much bigger things that are important for their families. These big arguments are called 'conflict'. But the good news is, many people are working very hard to help everyone understand each other better and find ways to live in peace. Peace means everyone is safe, happy, and shares kindly. We all want peace, don't we? Did you know? Nigeria is the most populated country in Africa! That means lots and lots of people live there, speaking many different languages and sharing amazing cultures.

Why Do People Fight?

Sometimes, people in Plateau State fight because they don't agree on some important things. It's like when you and your friend both want the last cookie. But for grown-ups, these disagreements can be about land, about their animals, or even about feeling like they belong to different groups. Let's learn more about these things.

Who Owns the Land?

Think about your home. That's your special place, right? In Plateau State, land is very, very important. It's where people build their homes, play with their friends, and grow yummy food like maize, yams, and vegetables. Farmers need land to grow crops to feed their families and sell some to earn money. But sometimes, two different groups of people might think a piece of land belongs to them. When they both want the same land, it can lead to big arguments. It's like two friends wanting the same special spot to build a sandcastle, but much more serious because it affects where they live and what they eat. Did you know? Farmers in Nigeria grow many delicious foods that help feed not only their families but also other people around the country!

Our Animal Friends: Cattle!

Many people in Plateau State have lovely animals called cattle, which are like big cows. These cows are super important! They give milk to drink and meat to eat, and some people sell them at the market to earn money for their families. People who herd cattle are called herders, and they often move their cows to find fresh grass and water. But here's where a problem can start: sometimes, these cows might walk onto a farmer's land and eat the crops! Imagine someone's cow eating all the corn you worked hard to grow. This can make farmers very upset, and then the herders and farmers might start to argue. Both groups are just trying to take care of their families, but sometimes their needs bump into each other. Did you know? In some parts of Africa, a person's wealth is measured by how many cattle they own! They are very precious animals.

Being Different but Still Friends

Have you ever noticed that everyone is special and unique? Some kids like to play football, others like to draw, and some speak different languages at home. In Plateau State, people belong to different groups. They might have different traditions, wear different clothes, or speak different languages. This is a wonderful thing, making Nigeria very colourful and diverse! But sometimes, people focus too much on these differences instead of remembering that everyone is a human being and should be treated with kindness. When people forget to be friendly to those who are different, it can make them feel like 'us' versus 'them', and that can lead to arguments and even fighting. It's important to remember that we are all friends and part of one big family, no matter what our differences are. Did you know? Nigeria has over 250 ethnic groups, each with its own beautiful culture and traditions! Isn't that amazing?

When Things Aren't Fair

When big arguments happen, we need grown-ups to help make things fair, right? We need someone to listen to everyone, understand both sides, and help fix the problem so everyone feels happy again. This is called 'justice'. It means that everyone gets a fair chance, and problems are solved in a way that is right for everyone.

The Grown-ups Who Help

In Plateau State, just like everywhere else, there are grown-ups whose job it is to help keep the peace and fix problems. These grown-ups are like the police or village leaders. They are supposed to help everyone feel safe and make sure things are fair. When people trust these helpers, they feel better about solving their problems. But sometimes, people in Plateau State don't feel like these grown-ups are always fair or can really help them. If people don't trust the helpers, they might try to fix problems themselves, which can make the arguments even bigger and more serious. It's very important that everyone feels like the grown-ups who are meant to help are doing a good job and are fair to all. Did you know? Being fair means treating everyone equally and giving everyone a chance to speak and be heard. It's a very important part of friendship and peace!

How Can We Make Peace?

Making peace is like growing a tiny seed into a big, strong plant. It takes time, care, and lots and lots of patience. It doesn't happen overnight! As the wise people say, 'Peace is a gradual thing.' This means we have to work on it little by little, every single day. Many wonderful people in Plateau State are working very hard to make peace happen. They are teaching people to talk kindly to each other, to share their problems, and to try and understand how others feel. They are helping farmers and herders sit down together and find ways to share the land and take care of their animals without arguing. Did you know? Talking kindly and listening carefully to others is a superpower! It helps us make new friends and solve problems without fighting.

What Can We Learn?

This story from Plateau State teaches us something very important: even when things are difficult, we can always find ways to make peace. It's about being kind, sharing, and remembering that we are all part of one big, human family. When we talk, listen, and try to understand each other, we can solve almost any problem. Even you, my little friends, can help make our world more peaceful! You can be kind to everyone at school, share your toys, and always try to understand your friends. If you see someone sad, give them a hug or ask if they need help. Every little act of kindness you do helps build a stronger, happier, and more peaceful world for everyone. Keep shining your light, my dear friends! Did you know? The more kind acts we do, the happier we feel inside, and the happier the people around us become too! Kindness is contagious!

originalTitle: Rwanda batter becomes youngest women's T20 centurion
title: Rwanda batter becomes youngest women's T20 centurion
content: Rwanda's Fanny Utagushimaninde says it was like "a dream" after becoming the youngest woman to make a T20 international century at the age of 15 years and 223 days.

originalTitle: Leading anti‑racism activist in Tunisia jailed for eight years
title: A Big Heart for Everyone: A Story from Tunisia
content:
Hello, Friends!

Hi everyone! It's your friendly news reporter for Africa, here to share a story with you. Today, we're going to talk about a very kind lady from a country called Tunisia. Tunisia is a beautiful country in North Africa. It has sunny beaches, yummy dates, and old, old cities!

Meet Saadia Mosbah, the Helper

There's a lady named Saadia Mosbah. Saadia has a very big heart. She loves to help people. She works very hard to make sure everyone is treated kindly and fairly. She believes that no one should be mean to another person because of their skin colour or where they come from. This is called being "anti-racism." Racism is when someone is unfair or unkind to another person just because they look different or come from a different place. It's really not nice at all! Saadia wants everyone to be friends.

What are "Migrants"?

Saadia helps special friends called "migrants." Do you know what a migrant is? A migrant is a person who moves from one home to another. Sometimes they move to a new city, or even a new country. They might move because they want to find a safe place to live, or maybe they are looking for a job so they can buy food and clothes for their family. Imagine moving to a brand-new school! You might feel a little bit shy at first, and you would want new friends to welcome you. That's a bit like what migrants feel. They are looking for a new place to call home and new friends to be kind to them. Did you know? People have moved from one place to another for thousands and thousands of years! It's how many countries all over the world became so colourful and full of different people and amazing stories!

Why Saadia Helped So Much

Saadia saw that many of these new friends, the migrants, needed help. They sometimes didn't have a place to sleep or enough food to eat. They might have felt lonely or scared. So, Saadia stepped up! She started campaigns. A campaign is when a group of people work together to tell everyone about something important. Saadia's campaign was to help migrants. She wanted to make sure they were safe and happy. She talked to people and tried to find homes and help for them. She wanted everyone to remember that all people deserve kindness and respect.

What the President Said

Now, in Tunisia, there is a very important leader called the President. His name is Kais Saied. The President said something that made some people feel worried. He said that the migrants were a "demographic threat" to the country. That's a big word! It means he thought that having too many people from other countries might cause problems for Tunisia's own people. He thought it might change the country too much. This made some people feel afraid of the migrants. It made it harder for the migrants to find a warm welcome.

Saadia Kept Helping

Even when the President said those things, Saadia didn't stop. She believed it was super important to keep helping. She knew that these people were just looking for a safe and happy life, just like you and me. She kept speaking up for them. She kept saying, "Everyone deserves kindness! Everyone deserves a safe home!" She kept working hard, even when it was difficult. She was very brave.

Sad News for Saadia

But then, something very sad happened. Because Saadia kept helping and kept speaking up for the migrants, the government said she did something wrong. They said she broke a rule. We don't know all the grown-up details, but it meant that they put her in jail. Jail is a place where people have to go if they break important rules. It's not a happy place. The government said Saadia has to stay in jail for a very, very long time – for eight years. That's a super long time, like when you are seven years old, and you have to wait until you are fifteen years old to see your family again. It's very sad news.

Why This Matters to All of Us

This story makes us feel a little bit sad, right? Because a very kind person, a helper, is now in a difficult place. Saadia was trying to make the world fairer and kinder. When people like Saadia are not free to help others, it makes us think. It makes us think about fairness. It makes us think about kindness. It makes us think about how we treat people who are new or different from us. Did you know? Every single person on Earth is part of the big human family! We all share this amazing planet.

What Can We Learn?

Even though this is a sad story, we can still learn something important from Saadia. We can learn to have a big heart, just like her. We can learn to be kind to everyone we meet, no matter where they come from, what they look like, or what language they speak. If we see someone who needs help, we can try to help them. Even small acts of kindness, like sharing a toy, saying a kind word, or making a new friend feel welcome, can make a huge difference. Imagine if everyone in Africa, and all over the world, tried to be a little bit more like Saadia. What a wonderful, kind, and friendly world it would be! Let's remember Saadia's kindness and try to be helpers in our own lives, every single day. We can make our corner of Africa, and the whole world, a better place, one kind act at a time. Did you know? Africa is a huge continent with many different countries and cultures! It's like a giant patchwork quilt of amazing people and stories!
summary: A kind lady named Saadia Mosbah from Tunisia, who helped people new to the country, has been put in jail for a long time.
whyMatters: It's important to remember that being kind and fair to everyone, especially people who need help, is very important, and we should always try to do what is right.

originalTitle: Drone footage captures flooding across Oahu, Hawaii
title: Drone footage captures flooding across Oahu, Hawaii
content: Drone footage shows flooding across Oahu’s North Shore as a second Kona Low storm hits Hawaii.

originalTitle: Evloev upsets Murphy, sets up featherweight title shot against Volkanovski
title: Movsar's Amazing Win: A Story of Never Giving Up!
content:
Hello, amazing kids of Africa! Your kid-friendly news reporter is here with a super exciting story from far, far away! It’s a story about strength, determination, and never giving up, even when things get a little tricky. We are going to talk about two very strong and brave athletes named Movsar and Lerone. They had a big, big competition in a huge city called London.

Meet Movsar and Lerone!

Movsar is a wonderful athlete. What is an athlete? It's someone who trains their body to be super strong and fast for sports! He works incredibly hard every single day. He runs, he jumps, and he learns many clever moves to be the best he can be. Lerone is also a fantastic athlete. He was super special because he had never, ever lost a competition before! Imagine playing a game and always winning. That's how good Lerone was! Everyone thought he was almost unbeatable. Did you know? Being an athlete means you eat healthy food like fruits and vegetables, and you get lots of sleep! This helps your body grow strong and stay healthy.

A Big Competition in London

The big competition where Movsar and Lerone played was held in a very famous city called London. London is very far away from our beautiful continent of Africa. You have to travel across a big blue ocean to get there! Imagine a giant stadium, full of bright lights and thousands of people cheering very, very loudly! Everyone was so excited to watch Movsar and Lerone show off their amazing skills. It was a day full of energy and anticipation.

A Tricky Moment

During the competition, something a little tricky happened to Movsar. Sometimes in sports, players make a small mistake. It’s not a bad mistake, just something that doesn't follow all the rules of the game exactly. Because of this tiny mistake, the people who judge the game had to do something. They said, "Oh no, Movsar, you will lose some points." Losing points is like when you're playing a game with your friends, and if you do something wrong, you might lose a turn or a point. It makes it harder to win. Did you know? Rules in games are very important! They help everyone play fair and stay safe. It's like the rules we have in school to help everyone learn and be happy.

Movsar's Brave Heart

When Movsar lost points, it could have made him feel sad or want to give up. But Movsar has a very strong heart and a very strong mind! He thought, "No! I will not give up! I will try even harder!" He remembered all the hours and hours he spent training. He pushed himself. He ran faster. He made his moves even more clever. He didn't let the lost points stop him. This is called determination! Determination means you really, really want to achieve something, and you work extra hard until you do it. Movsar showed everyone what a determined person he is! Did you know? Many heroes from Africa, like the great Nelson Mandela, showed amazing determination to make our world a better place. They never gave up on their dreams!

Movsar Wins!

Movsar kept trying and trying. He used all his strength and all his smart tactics. And guess what happened? He won the competition! He actually won against Lerone, the athlete who had never lost before! Everyone in the stadium cheered loudly for Movsar! He showed everyone that even if things get tough and you make a mistake, you can still win if you have a brave heart and never, ever give up. It was a truly amazing victory!

A Chance for a Super Belt!

Because Movsar won this very big and important game, he gets a super special prize! He now has a chance to compete for something called a "featherweight title." What is a title? Imagine a big, shiny, golden belt that says you are the very, very best in the world at your sport! It means you are a champion! Movsar will now get to play against another super strong athlete named Volkanovski. This will be an even bigger game, for the golden belt that shows who is the top champion in his category! Imagine being the champion of the whole world in your favourite sport! That's what Movsar is aiming for now. Did you know? In many sports across Africa, like soccer or rugby, the winning team also gets a big trophy or a cup to show they are the champions!

Be Like Movsar!

So, what can we learn from Movsar's amazing story, kids? First, always try your very best, even when things feel hard. Second, don't give up if you make a mistake; learn from it and keep trying! Third, work hard every day to get better at what you love. Movsar's story is a happy story about being brave, being strong, and never, ever giving up on your dreams! Let's all cheer for Movsar and wish him good luck for his next big game to win that golden champion's belt! He is a true inspiration!
summary: Movsar Evloev, a determined athlete, won a big competition in London against Lerone Murphy, who had never lost before, even after Movsar lost some points, and now Movsar gets to compete for a special champion's belt.
whyMatters: This story is important because it teaches us to never give up, even when things are difficult, and to always try our best, just like Movsar did to win his big game and get a chance to become a super champion.

originalTitle: Trump threatens to ‘obliterate’ Iran power plants unless Hormuz Strait open
title: A Friendly Chat About News From Far Away
content:
Hello, Super Friends!

Hello, amazing young explorers and future leaders of Africa! It's your friendly kid-friendly news reporter here, ready to share some important news from a land far, far away. We're going to talk about some grown-up news, but don't worry, we'll make it super easy to understand, just like a storybook. Sometimes, grown-ups in different countries don't always agree, and they might say things that sound a bit big or scary. Our job today is to understand what's happening and remember that many people are always working for peace and kindness.

Who is Donald Trump?

First, let's meet one of the grown-ups in our news story. His name is Donald Trump. He used to be the 'President' of a very big country called America, also known as the United States of America. A President is like the head teacher of a whole country! They help make big decisions for everyone living there. Donald Trump is a very famous person, and he often talks about what he thinks is important for his country. He's not the President anymore, but people still talk about things he said when he was. Think of him as a leader who made big choices for his country a few years ago. Did you know? The United States is a country far away across the big ocean! It has many famous cities and tall buildings, just like some places in Africa.

Where is Iran?

Now, let's learn about another country in our story: Iran. Iran is also a country far away from Africa, on a continent called Asia. It's a land with a very long history, beautiful gardens, and kind people. Tehran is the name of Iran's capital city, which is like the main city where the government works, just like Abuja in Nigeria or Pretoria in South Africa. Imagine Iran as a special country with its own culture, songs, and food. Just like you have friends from different countries in Africa, there are people in Iran who live their lives, go to school, and play, too! Did you know? Iran has some very old and amazing buildings called mosques, which are places where people go to pray. Some of them are hundreds of years old!

What is a "Threat"?

In our news story, it says Donald Trump 'threatened' Iran. What does 'threaten' mean? Well, imagine if you told your friend, "If you don't share your toys, I won't play with you!" That's a little bit like a threat. It means saying something bad might happen if someone doesn't do what you want them to do. In the news, it means Mr. Trump said something very strong. He said he would 'obliterate' Iran's power plants. 'Obliterate' is a big word for 'destroy very, very badly.' It's a scary thought, right? But remember, grown-ups often use strong words when they are trying to solve big problems, even if it's not the nicest way to talk. Did you know? When grown-ups talk to each other to solve problems, it's called 'diplomacy.' It's like talking things out nicely instead of arguing loudly!

What are Power Plants?

Now, let's talk about 'power plants.' This sounds like something out of a science fiction movie, but it's really important for our everyday lives! A power plant is a big building or group of buildings where we make electricity. Think of all the things electricity does for you: it lights up your house, charges your phone, makes your TV work, and keeps your food cold in the fridge! Power plants use different things, like water, wind, sunlight, or even special fuel, to create the electricity that travels through wires to our homes. If a power plant stops working, then a lot of people would lose their lights and many other things we use every day. That's why they are so important! Did you know? The first power plant that gave electricity to many homes was built a very long time ago, in 1882, in a city called New York in America!

What is the Strait of Hormuz?

This is a tricky name, but it's actually a very important place! Imagine a very busy, narrow road, but instead of cars, it's full of giant ships! The 'Strait of Hormuz' is a narrow path of water between two big pieces of land. It connects a big ocean to a smaller sea. Think of it like a very important highway for boats. Many, many big ships have to sail through this narrow water path every single day to get from one part of the world to another. It's like the main gate for ships carrying important goods. Did you know? A 'strait' is a natural narrow waterway that connects two larger bodies of water. It's like a watery shortcut!

Why is the Strait of Hormuz Important?

So, why is this narrow water road so important? It's mostly because of something called 'oil.' Oil is a thick, dark liquid that comes from deep underground. We use oil for many things! It's the fuel that makes cars, buses, and aeroplanes go. It also helps make electricity in some power plants, and it's used to make plastics and other things we use every day. Many countries around the world need oil to keep their cars running, their lights on, and their factories working. A lot of this oil comes from countries near Iran, and almost all of it travels through the Strait of Hormuz on big ships called oil tankers. So, if this 'watery road' gets blocked, it means many countries won't get the oil they need, and that can cause big problems. Did you know? One big oil tanker can carry enough oil to fill thousands of cars! They are truly gigantic ships.

Why Are They Talking About "Opening" It?

Sometimes, countries have disagreements. In the past, Iran has sometimes said that they might stop ships from going through the Strait of Hormuz. When countries say they might 'close' the strait, it means they might try to stop the ships carrying oil. Other countries, like America, don't want that to happen because they need the oil to keep their own countries running. So, when Donald Trump talked about Iran needing to 'open' the Strait of Hormuz, he meant that ships should be allowed to pass freely without any problems. He was saying that if Iran tried to stop the ships, then there would be serious consequences.

Why is Talking Better Than Fighting?

It's important to remember that when grown-ups talk about 'threats' or 'destroying' things, it's usually because they are trying to get other grown-ups to agree with them. But the best way for people and countries to solve problems is by talking to each other with calm voices and listening carefully, just like you learn to do with your friends and family. Fighting or hurting things never makes anyone truly happy in the end. It often makes things worse for everyone. That's why leaders all over the world usually try very hard to talk and find peaceful solutions to big disagreements. It's called 'negotiation' or 'diplomacy,' and it's much better than making threats. Did you know? Many smart grown-ups, called diplomats, spend their whole lives helping countries talk to each other to avoid fights and build friendships!

What Does This Mean for Us in Africa?

Now, you might be thinking, "What does this news from far away mean for me here in Africa?" Well, the good news is that this news is happening very, very far from our wonderful continent. Our leaders and countries in Africa are always working hard to keep our homes safe and peaceful. While big news like this can sound a bit scary, it's important to know that many, many grown-ups all over the world are always trying to find ways for everyone to live together peacefully. This news reminds us how important it is for everyone, no matter where they are from, to try and understand each other and solve problems with words, not anger.

Let's Remember Peace!

So, my fantastic friends, what we've learned today is about leaders, faraway countries, important water paths, and why talking is always better than making threats. Even when grown-ups say big, strong words, there are always people working to keep the world safe and fair for everyone, especially for amazing kids like you! Let's all remember to be kind to our friends, listen to each other, and always choose peace. That's the best news we can make every single day. Keep exploring, keep learning, and keep shining bright!
summary: Former US President Donald Trump once made a strong statement, threatening to damage Iran's electricity-making places if Iran stopped ships from using a vital narrow water path called the Strait of Hormuz, which is very important for moving oil around the world.
whyMatters: This news is important because it talks about how grown-ups from different countries sometimes disagree very strongly, and it highlights a narrow water path called the Strait of Hormuz that is super important for big ships carrying oil to many places, including Africa, which helps power our cars and lights.

originalTitle: Iranian strike hits near Israeli nuclear facility after Tehran says its site targeted
title: Talking About Peace: News From Far Away
content:
Hello, My Amazing Friends!

Jambo! It's your friendly reporter here, bringing you news from far, far away. Sometimes, grown-ups in different countries have big talks. We want to tell you about something that happened, but in a way that is easy to understand. Remember, it's very important to talk about peace and being kind to each other.

News From Distant Lands

Imagine our big, beautiful world! There are so many countries on it. Today, we're talking about two countries that are very far from our wonderful home in Africa. One country is called Iran, and the other is called Israel. They are both in a part of the world that is many, many kilometres away from us.

What Happened?

Sometimes, countries have big disagreements. Recently, grown-ups in Iran said that something was hit near a special building in their country. Then, grown-ups in Israel said something happened near a special building in their country too. It's like when two children accidentally bump into each other, and then one bumps back. But this is much bigger and not a game. Did you know? Our Earth is so big that you could fit all of Africa, Iran, and Israel inside it many times over!

What is a Special Building? (A "Nuclear Facility")

Now, let's talk about those "special buildings." They are called "nuclear facilities." That's a big, fancy word! Think of it like a very big science lab. In these labs, clever scientists work with tiny, tiny things called "atoms." Atoms are like the super small building blocks of everything around us – your toys, the trees, even you! Scientists can use atoms to make electricity for homes and schools, or to help make special medicines. It's a very powerful science. Did you know? Atoms are so small that you cannot see them, even with your super strong eyes! You need a very special microscope to see them.

Why These Buildings are Important

These special buildings are very important because the work inside them is powerful. It's like how we use powerful electricity to light our homes. We need to be very careful with powerful things. That's why grown-ups want to make sure these special buildings are always used for good, peaceful things.

Who Helps Keep Things Safe? (The IAEA)

There's a super important group of grown-ups from all over the world. They are like peacekeepers or referees for these special buildings. This group is called the "International Atomic Energy Agency." (You can just call them the "Peacekeeper Agency" for short!) Their main job is to visit these special buildings in many countries. They check to make sure everything is being done safely and for peaceful reasons, like making electricity, not for anything that could cause problems. Did you know? The Peacekeeper Agency has scientists and inspectors from many different countries, all working together to help keep our world safe!

What the Boss Said

The main boss, or "chief," of this Peacekeeper Agency is a very wise grown-up. After hearing about the things that happened far away, he spoke up. He said something really important: he asked everyone to show "maximum military restraint." What does "maximum military restraint" mean? It's a big way of saying: "Please, grown-ups, stop everything right now! Be super, super careful and don't do anything else that might make things worse. Let's talk instead!" Did you know? Talking things through is almost always the best way to solve a problem, just like you talk with your friends when there's a disagreement.

Why is This Important for Peace?

The boss of the Peacekeeper Agency wants to make sure that countries don't keep doing things that could make bigger problems. He wants everyone to be calm and think before acting. This helps keep everyone safe and keeps peace in the world. Just like in your school, if everyone is kind and tries to solve problems by talking, it makes for a happy, safe place. The same is true for countries!

No Worries Here in Africa!

My dear friends, it's very important to know that these things are happening very, very far away from our beautiful continent of Africa. Our homes and schools here are safe. This news is about grown-ups in countries far away. We in Africa always hope for peace everywhere. We love our vibrant cultures, our amazing animals, and our happy communities. We send our wishes for peace to all corners of the world. Did you know? Africa is home to some of the most incredible animals on Earth, like elephants, lions, and giraffes! They all live peacefully together in their habitats.

Let's Hope for Peace

So, what did we learn today? We learned that grown-ups in different countries sometimes have disagreements. But there are also wonderful grown-ups, like the Peacekeeper Agency, who work very hard to keep everyone safe and help countries talk to each other instead of fighting. Let's all hope that the grown-ups in Iran and Israel listen to the wise words of the Peacekeeper Agency. Let's hope they choose to be extra careful and talk things through. Because peace is the best way for everyone to live happily ever after! Keep smiling, my amazing friends!
summary: A global group that helps keep peace asked two countries far away to be careful and talk instead of fighting, especially about special science buildings.
whyMatters: This is important because everyone wants countries to be peaceful and safe, and to make sure powerful science is used for good things, not for problems.

originalTitle: Socialists battle to hold Paris as France holds mayoral elections
title: A Big Election in Paris: Choosing a New City Leader!
content:
Hello, Super Friends!

Hello, hello, my super friends across Africa! It's your friendly news reporter here. I'm so excited to tell you about something very important happening in a faraway city. This city is called Paris. It is the capital city of a country called France. France is in a continent called Europe, which is very far from us! But even far away, things happen that are interesting to learn about. Today, we are talking about choosing leaders. Just like your school has a head teacher, and your country has a president, big cities also have special leaders. They are called Mayors. Let’s learn all about a big election that just happened in Paris!

What is a Mayor?

Imagine you live in a big, busy city. Who makes sure the roads are smooth for cars and bicycles? Who helps build fun playgrounds for you to play in? Who makes sure the rubbish is collected so the city stays clean? That person is often the Mayor! Did you know? A Mayor is like the captain of a very big team – the city team! They help make decisions to make the city a better place for everyone who lives there. They listen to what people need and try to fix problems. It's a very important job! Mayors also help with schools, hospitals, and parks. They want the city to be safe and happy. In Paris, the Mayor has a very, very big job because Paris is a huge city with millions of people living in it. It's a bit like being the head of a very, very big family!

Meeting Paris, the City of Lights!

Paris is one of the most famous cities in the whole world! People call it the 'City of Lights' because it has so many beautiful bright lights at night. It's also famous for amazing buildings like the Eiffel Tower. Have you ever seen a picture of the Eiffel Tower? It’s super tall and magnificent! Paris is also known for yummy food like croissants and for beautiful art. Many tourists from all over the world love to visit Paris. It is a very old city with lots of history, like many cities in Africa. But even old cities need new ideas and new leaders sometimes. Did you know? The Eiffel Tower is almost 330 metres tall! That's like stacking over 100 elephants on top of each other! Imagine that! It's truly an amazing structure in Paris. The Mayor of Paris helps look after wonderful places like the Eiffel Tower and the parks around it.

Choosing Our Leaders: What is an Election?

So, how do people choose their Mayor? They do it with something called an election. An election is when grown-ups, like your parents or older brothers and sisters, get to vote. Voting is like choosing your favourite colour or your favourite game. Everyone gets a chance to say who they want to be the leader. When people vote, they mark a paper to show who they think will do the best job. Then, all the votes are counted very carefully. The person with the most votes wins! That person then becomes the Mayor and gets to lead the city for a few years. It's a fair way to choose who gets to be in charge. Did you know? In most countries, you have to be at least 18 years old to vote. This is because grown-ups are expected to understand all the different ideas from the people who want to be leaders. Your vote is your voice, and it helps decide the future of your city and country.

The Big Race for Paris!

In Paris, there was a very important election recently. Two main people wanted to be the Mayor. Their names are Rachida Dati and Emmanuel Grégoire. Think of it like a big race to see who will be the city's next top leader! They both wanted to show people that they had the best ideas for Paris. One of them, Emmanuel Grégoire, belongs to a group called the 'Socialists'. This group has been in charge of Paris for a very long time – 25 years! Imagine that, almost as long as your parents have been alive! They have been making decisions for Paris for a quarter of a century. But this time, Rachida Dati from another group was trying to win. It was a close race! Everyone in Paris was very excited to see who would win and lead their beautiful city. It's like waiting to see who wins a big football match, but for the whole city!

Different Teams, Different Ideas

In elections, people who want to lead often belong to different 'teams' or 'parties'. These teams have different ideas about how to run a city. The 'Socialists' team, who have been leading Paris, have one way of doing things. They often focus on helping everyone equally and making sure everyone has a chance to succeed. The other team, which Rachida Dati belongs to, is sometimes called the 'right'. They might have different ideas. Maybe they focus more on businesses or on different ways to manage money. It’s important to remember that both teams want to make Paris a good city, but they have different ideas about the best way to do it. Did you know? In many countries, there are many different political 'teams' or parties. Each team has special colours or symbols to represent their ideas. It's a way for people to quickly understand what each group stands for. It's like having different uniforms for different sports teams.

What Happens When a City Changes its Leader?

If Rachida Dati wins, it would mean a big change for Paris. After 25 years with one team, a new team would be in charge. This means new ideas, new plans, and maybe even new projects for the city. It’s like when a new teacher comes to your class – things might be a little different! The new Mayor might decide to build new parks, change how traffic works, or even make new rules about keeping the city clean. These changes can affect everyone living in Paris, from the oldest grandparent to the youngest child. Change can be exciting! It brings new energy and new ways of looking at old problems. Sometimes, a fresh start is exactly what a big city needs to keep growing and improving for everyone. People vote for change when they want to try something new.

Why Does This Matter to Kids?

Even though kids don't get to vote, what a Mayor does matters a lot to you! If the Mayor decides to build more playgrounds, that’s great for playtime! If they make sure schools are safe and have good books, that helps you learn and grow. If the city is clean and green, you have nicer places to play outside. If there are good roads, it's easier for your parents to get to work or take you to fun places. So, even though this election happened far away in Paris, the idea of choosing good leaders is important for kids everywhere, including right here in Africa! Did you know? Mayors often have special programs just for children and families. They want to make sure the youngest people in their city are happy and healthy because kids are the future of the city! So, their decisions are super important.

Bye for Now!

Well, my amazing friends, that's our news for today from Paris! It reminds us that choosing leaders is a very important job everywhere. From our biggest cities in Africa to famous cities like Paris, everyone works together to make their homes the best they can be. Keep learning and keep curious! Your friendly news reporter will be back with more exciting stories soon. Bye-bye!
summary: In Paris, France, people held an election to choose a new Mayor, and after 25 years, the city might get a new leader from a different group.
whyMatters: This election matters because the Mayor helps decide important things like building schools, parks, and keeping the city clean, which affects everyone, even kids.

originalTitle: Did Australia's under-16s social media ban work?
title: Australia's Big Rule: Kids and Social Media!
content:
Jambo, amazing kids of Africa! I'm your friendly news reporter, here to share a very interesting story from a country far, far away. It's called Australia! They have made a new rule about how young people use social media, and we're going to talk about it today.

What is Social Media?

Imagine you have a magic phone or a computer. Social media is like a special place on that magic screen where you can share pictures, videos, and messages with your friends and family. It's how many people talk to each other, play games, and see what their friends are doing, even if they are far away. Did you know? Apps like TikTok, Instagram, and Facebook are all kinds of social media! Sometimes, grown-ups worry that kids spend too much time looking at these screens. They worry about what kids might see online, or if they are missing out on other fun things.

What is a "Ban"?

A "ban" means to stop something from happening. It's like when your mom says, "No eating sweets before dinner!" That's a little ban! In Australia, they made a rule, or a 'ban,' that young people under a certain age cannot use social media by themselves. Did you know? This rule is for kids who are 15 years old or younger. That's a big age! This ban is a special law. It means that if you are under 16 in Australia, you can't just open a social media app and start using it whenever you want. Grown-ups are trying to help kids be safe and happy.

Why Did Australia Do This?

The grown-ups in Australia thought a lot about kids and their screens. They saw that some kids were spending many hours looking at social media. They worried that this was not good for their eyes, their brains, or for playing outside. Did you know? Spending too much time looking at screens can make your eyes feel tired! They also wanted to make sure kids were safe. Sometimes, things on the internet can be confusing or even a little bit scary. This rule is like having an extra big hug to keep kids safe online.

How Does This Rule Work?

So, if you are under 16 in Australia, you need a grown-up to help you use social media. It means parents or guardians have to agree and help you set up an account. They can also help decide how much time you spend on it. Did you know? Australia was the very first country in the world to make a rule like this for young people! This rule tries to help kids have a better balance. Balance means having time for screens AND time for playing, reading, and talking to people in person. It's like having a healthy meal with different kinds of food.

What Happened After Three Months?

The new rule has been in Australia for three months already! That's a whole quarter of a year. Reporters talked to some teenagers to see how they felt about it. These teenagers are between 13 and 15 years old. Did you know? Three months is a long time to try something new! Some kids said it was a little hard at first. They missed chatting with their friends online. But others said they found new ways to have fun! They started doing different things instead of just looking at their phones.

The Good Things About the Ban

Many kids found good things! Some said they spent more time playing outside, running around, and enjoying the sunshine. Others started reading more books, which is a wonderful way to go on adventures without leaving your home. Did you know? Playing outside helps your body grow strong and makes you happy! Some kids also said they talked more to their family at home. They had more time for board games, telling stories, or just laughing together. It made them feel more connected to the people right next to them. They also felt less pressure. Sometimes on social media, kids feel like they need to look perfect or have lots of friends. With less social media, some felt more relaxed and like themselves.

The Tricky Things About the Ban

Of course, some things were a little tricky. Some teenagers said they missed seeing what their friends were doing. It felt like they were missing out on jokes or plans. Did you know? Friendship is very important, and it's good to talk to your friends in many ways! It was harder for them to quickly message a friend to ask about homework or to just say hello. They had to find other ways to connect, like calling or seeing each other at school. Finding new ways can be hard at first.

What Can We Learn from Australia?

Even if this rule isn't in our country, we can still learn from Australia! It makes us think about how much time we spend on screens. It's good to think about if we are playing enough, reading enough, and talking to our families enough. Did you know? Your brain needs a break from screens, just like your body needs a break from running! We can all try to find a good balance. A balance where we can enjoy our screens sometimes, but also have lots of time for other fun things that make us smile and learn.

Tips for You: Be Smart Online!

Here are some tips for you, my bright young friends: 1. **Ask a grown-up:** Always ask your mom, dad, or another trusted grown-up before you go online or use any new app. 2. **Set limits:** Talk with your grown-up about how much screen time is okay for you each day. Maybe 30 minutes, maybe an hour, then go play! 3. **Play outside:** Make sure you spend time running, jumping, and playing with your friends in the fresh air. 4. **Read books:** Books are amazing! They can take you to magical lands and teach you new things. 5. **Talk to your family:** Share your day, tell jokes, and listen to stories from your family. It's super important! Did you know? Always keep your personal information, like your full name or where you live, a secret online! Only share with grown-ups you trust in person. So, my wonderful listeners, the story from Australia teaches us to be smart and balanced with our screens. It's all about keeping you happy, healthy, and safe! Until next time, stay curious and keep shining brightly!
summary: Australia has a new rule for kids under 16 that limits their social media use, helping them spend more time playing, reading, and being with family, even though some miss chatting with friends online.
whyMatters: This is important because it helps kids be safe online and makes sure they have enough time to play outside, read books, and talk to their families, which are all very good for growing up happy and healthy.

title: Who needs cup final victory most - Arteta or Guardiola?
content: Premier League leaders Arsenal face title rivals Manchester City in the EFL Cup final on Sunday, but how important could the result prove in title race?

originalTitle: Outrun and in decline - are Rosenior's Chelsea near crisis point?
title: Our Friends, Chelsea Football Team, Are Taking a Break to Get Stronger!
content:
Hello, hello, amazing friends all across Africa! It’s your friendly news reporter, coming to you with some fun news about a very famous football team. Imagine a team of brave players, just like a group of friends, who run very fast on a big green field, chasing a round ball. This team is called Chelsea! Many of you might know them or cheer for them when they play on TV.

Sometimes, when we play games, our team wins, and sometimes the other team wins. That's just part of playing! Our friends, the Chelsea team, played a game recently against another team called Everton. It was a very exciting game, but on that day, the Everton team scored more goals than Chelsea. That means Everton won the game.

It's okay to not win every time. Just like when you play a game with your friends and someone else wins, you learn, you smile, and you get ready to play again! The Chelsea team tried very, very hard, running all over the field, kicking the ball, and working together. That’s the most important part: trying your best and working with your teammates.

What Happened on the Big Green Field?


Picture a big, beautiful stadium with lots of people cheering! That's where the game happened. Chelsea was playing far away from their home stadium. The players ran with all their might, passed the ball to each other, and tried to get it into the other team's net. When the ball goes into the net, it's called a goal! The Everton team scored three goals that day. Our Chelsea friends tried their best to score too, but the other team's goalkeeper was very good at stopping the ball.

It can feel a little bit sad when your team doesn't win, right? But the players never stop trying until the very last whistle blows. They keep running, keep tackling (that means trying to get the ball from the other team fairly), and keep encouraging each other. This is what makes football so exciting – every player gives their very best!

Did you know? A football game has two halves, each lasting 45 minutes! That's a lot of running for the players! They get a short break in the middle to rest and talk to their coach.

Time for a Little Holiday and Practice!


Now, after playing many, many games, the Chelsea team is getting a special break. It's like when you have a holiday from school or a weekend break from playing. This break is called an 'international break'.

What does 'international break' mean? It means that some of the players, who come from different countries around the world, go to play for their home country's team. It's a very special honor for them! And the rest of the Chelsea team gets to rest and practice extra hard without playing big games for two whole weeks. Two weeks is a long time, right?

What do you think they do during this break? Do they just sit around and do nothing? No way! They use this time to think. They think about how they played in their last games. They watch videos of themselves playing, like watching a movie of their own game. This helps them see what they did really well and what they can do even better next time.

Their coach, who is like a very wise teacher for the team, will help them. The coach will teach them new moves, new ways to pass the ball, and new ways to work together as a team. It's like when your teacher helps you learn new things in class so you can get smarter and better at your schoolwork. They will be practicing, practicing, practicing!

Did you know? Each football team usually has 11 players on the field at one time, all working together to score goals and protect their own net!

Learning from Every Game


Every game, whether you win or lose, is a chance to learn. Imagine you're building a tower with blocks, and sometimes it falls down. Do you give up? No! You learn how to make it stronger next time, right? It's the same for football players.

The Chelsea players are very strong and determined. They know that even the best teams sometimes have days where things don't go perfectly. This break gives them a wonderful chance to get stronger, smarter, and even more ready for their next big challenge. They will talk to each other, encourage each other, and promise to keep trying their very best.

We, as fans, always cheer for our teams. We know they are doing their very best. And the most important thing is that they keep playing with heart, with passion, and with a smile. They are working hard to make all their supporters happy.

Did you know? The football itself is usually made of leather or a special synthetic material and is kicked all over the field! It must be round, of course!

Football Love in Africa!


Many, many kids just like you, all across Africa, love football! We play football in our villages, in our towns, in our schools. We gather with our families to watch the big games. We know that football is more than just a game; it brings people together, makes us cheer, and teaches us about teamwork and never giving up.

When Chelsea plays, they know that millions of people around the world, including lots of kids in Africa, are watching and cheering for them. That makes them want to try even harder!

Did you know? Many amazing football players from countries in Africa play for big teams in Europe, including some who have played for Chelsea! They make us all very proud!

Ready to Play Again Soon!


So, after their two-week break, our friends the Chelsea team will be back! They will have had time to rest, to practice, and to learn. They will be full of new energy, new ideas, and new determination. They will be ready to run onto that big green field again, kick the ball with all their might, and try their very best to score lots of goals.

We will be here, cheering for them, hoping they have lots of fun and play wonderful, exciting football. Remember, trying your best and never giving up is what truly matters in any game, and in life too! So let’s cheer for Chelsea as they get ready to shine brightly once more! Go, Chelsea, go! You can do it!
summary: The Chelsea football team had a tough game, but they are now taking a two-week break to rest, practice, and learn new things so they can come back stronger and ready to play their best for their next matches.
whyMatters: This matters because even when things are tough, like not winning a game, it's a chance to learn, practice, and try even harder next time, showing us all the importance of teamwork and never giving up.

originalTitle: 'If Liverpool play like that at PSG it could be 10-0'
title: Let's Play Football! A Big Game for Our Friends at Liverpool!
content:
Hi everyone! Do you love to play games? Maybe kick a ball with your friends? Football is a super fun game where two teams try to kick a ball into a net. It's called a goal! Today, we are talking about a famous football team called Liverpool. They wear bright red shirts, and many people love to watch them play! Did you know? Football is the most popular game in the whole wide world! So many kids and grown-ups love to play it, kick a ball, and cheer for their favourite teams!

What Happened Last Time?

Liverpool played a game recently. Sometimes, when we play a game or try something new, we don't always do our very best, right? It was a bit like that for Liverpool in their last game. They tried hard, but the ball didn't always go where they wanted it to. Imagine you are building a tall tower with blocks, and sometimes, a few blocks fall down. It can feel a little bit sad or tricky. That's how it can feel when a football team doesn't play as well as they hoped. But it's okay, because every game is a chance to learn! Did you know? In football, when one team scores more goals than the other team, they win! If they score the same number, it's called a draw, and both teams get a point!

Meet Our Coach, Mr. Arne Slot!

Every football team has a very special helper called a coach. A coach is like a teacher for the team, helping them learn and grow. Liverpool's new coach is a kind man named Mr. Arne Slot. His job is to help all the players learn new tricks and play their very best together. When the team didn't play so well last time, Mr. Slot thought, "Hmm, how can I help them do much better next time?" A coach tells the players what to do on the field. He shows them how to pass the ball to their friends and how to kick it to score amazing goals. It's very important for everyone on the team to listen to their coach. Just like you listen to your teachers at school to learn new things! Did you know? A good coach helps everyone on the team feel important and work together like one big, happy family! Teamwork makes the dream work in football and in life!

Getting Ready for a Big Game!

Soon, Liverpool will play another very, very big game. They will play against another super strong team called PSG. PSG is a famous team from a country far away called France. They also have super good players who are very fast and clever! To play against a strong team like PSG, Liverpool needs to practice extra hard. They need to get ready, just like you get ready for a big school show or a special sports day. What is practice? It's like doing your homework or playing your favourite musical instrument every day. Doing it again and again makes you better and better! The Liverpool players will run and kick and pass the ball to each other again and again. They will do it until they are super fast and super good. They will learn new ways to play together as a team. They will try to be super speedy and super smart with the ball! Mr. Slot, their coach, will teach them new plans and help them remember what to do in the game. He will make sure they are ready. Did you know? Some football players can kick the ball almost as far as a grown-up car is long! Wow! They have very strong legs!

Why Is Trying Our Best So Important?

Even if we don't win every single game we play, it's always, always important to try our very, very best. That's what really matters! When you try your best, you learn many new things. You get stronger and faster, and you become better at whatever you are doing. And most importantly, you have lots of fun with your friends, who are your teammates! Playing together is a happy thing to do. Football is also all about playing fair and being kind to everyone, even the players on the other team. Good sports shake hands! So, when Liverpool plays PSG, we hope they will play with big smiles and happy hearts. They will give it their all, trying their hardest for all their fans. They will run and jump and kick, chasing the ball and helping each other on the field. Did you know? When players shake hands before a game, it's a way of saying "Good luck!" and being friendly, no matter who wins or loses!

Let's Cheer for Liverpool!

We know Liverpool's coach, Mr. Slot, will help them prepare very well. We know the players will try super hard in practice. It's like when you study for a big test at school or practice for a school play. You get ready so you can do your best! We will cheer for Liverpool! We will hope they have a wonderful game and score many, many goals. Remember, playing football is about having fun, working together as a team, and never giving up, even when it's tough! Go, Liverpool, go! We believe in you! Have fun in your next game!
summary: Liverpool, a famous football team, had a difficult game, but their new coach, Mr. Arne Slot, is helping them practice hard and work together to play their very best in their next big match against PSG.
whyMatters: It shows us that even when things are tough, practicing and working as a team can help us get better and have fun, just like in school or games with friends, and that's a lesson for everyone!
